title: PillCam-SPECTRA Bleeding-Region Detection (PWDR)
sub_domain: Capsule-endoscopic bleeding / polyp / mucosal-anomaly classification via thresholded chromophore recovery
source_data_quality_note: First v2 physical_with_discrete_readout (PWDR) genesis Principle. Wraps L1-511 PillCam-SPECTRA Optical-Property Recovery (analytical core) with a deterministic threshold function on recovered chromophore concentrations to yield categorical labels {normal, bleeding, polyp, suspected_neoplasia, mucosal_inflammation}. The threshold is piecewise-constant and continuous (Lipschitz) on the underlying physical state, inheriting the analytical core's S1-S4 certificates. Per pwm-team/pwm_product/genesis/CLASSIFICATION_SEGMENTATION_PRINCIPLES.md (the canonical Option-3 recast pattern) and PWM_V2_GENESIS_INCLUSION.md (the v2 PWDR pilot pair). Establishes the v2 schema convention: gate_class='physical_with_discrete_readout', gate_substitutions populated, discrete_readout block, physics_core_principle link to L1-511.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on {normal, bleeding, polyp, suspected_neoplasia, mucosal_inflammation} categorical readout; permutation invariance enforced under tissue-region relabeling

discrete_readout.threshold_function: label(r) = argmax_class [score_class(c_HbO2(r), c_Hb(r), c_water(r), c_lipid(r), c_melanin(r))] where score_bleeding = 1[c_HbO2 > tau_HbO2 AND blood_volume_fraction > tau_BVF]; score_polyp = 1[c_lipid > tau_lipid_polyp AND mu_s' > tau_scatter_polyp]; score_suspected_neoplasia = 1[mu_s' > tau_scatter_neop AND c_HbO2/c_Hb > tau_oxy_ratio_neop]; score_mucosal_inflammation = 1[c_HbO2 + c_Hb > tau_total_blood AND c_water > tau_edema]; score_normal = complementary; thresholds tau_* derived from clinical-validation cohort or PWM benchmark calibration
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: tissue mucosal optical state (mu_a(lambda), mu_s'(lambda)) and derived chromophore concentrations (c_HbO2, c_Hb, c_water, c_lipid, c_melanin) recovered by the L1-511 analytical core
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Each per-class score is an indicator function of a Lipschitz-continuous combination of (c_chrom, mu_s'); argmax is locally Lipschitz-continuous on the open set where one class strictly dominates. Boundaries between classes form measure-zero hyperplanes in chromophore space; a smoothed-argmax (softmax with finite temperature) is globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.10 per unit chromophore-concentration range. Inherits underlying-state stability from L1-511 (kappa_eff ~ 15).

related_principles: L1-511 (PillCam-SPECTRA Optical-Property Recovery — analytical core, physics_core_principle)

E.description: PillCam-SPECTRA Bleeding-Region Detection (PWDR): the v2 physical_with_discrete_readout wrapper around L1-511 analytical core. The forward operator is the composition (threshold_function . L1-511_forward): multispectral capsule frames -> recovered chromophore concentrations (c_HbO2, c_Hb, c_water, c_lipid, c_melanin)(r) via L1-511 -> categorical labels {normal, bleeding, polyp, suspected_neoplasia, mucosal_inflammation}(r) via piecewise-constant threshold function on chromophore concentrations and reduced scattering coefficient mu_s'. The threshold is locally Lipschitz-continuous on the underlying physical state with global Lipschitz constant ~0.10 (per the smoothed-softmax formulation in discrete_readout.threshold_function). Recovery is posed as the joint problem: (i) recover continuous physical state via L1-511 (analytical S1-S4); (ii) apply deterministic threshold to produce categorical readout (S1-S4 inherited via Lipschitz continuity). The gate_substitutions field populates only conservation_substitute (softmax simplex closure on categorical readout); kappa_substitute and h_substitute are null because the physics core retains its analytical kappa and h. Difficulty tier delta = 3 inherited from L1-511. Mismatch parameters extend the L1-511 set: threshold_calibration_uncertainty, between-region_smoothing_artifact, motion-induced_label_flicker, capsule_orientation_mucosal_assignment.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-511): I_back(r, lambda) = I_0(lambda) * R_diffuse(mu_a(r, lambda), mu_s'(r, lambda), g, rho); recover (c_HbO2, c_Hb, c_water, c_lipid, c_melanin)(r) and mu_s'(r, lambda). Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): label(r) = argmax_class score_class(c, mu_s') per discrete_readout.threshold_function. Joint: classification(r) <- threshold( L1-511_recover(I_back) ).
E.world_state_x: Per-pixel categorical mucosal classification label(r) over {normal, bleeding, polyp, suspected_neoplasia, mucosal_inflammation}; semantic segmentation of the multispectral capsule frame.
E.observation_y: Multispectral capsule frame I_back(x, y, lambda) (same as L1-511); calibrated illumination spectrum I_0(lambda) and capsule geometry (rho, working distance) as constants.

G.dag: L.optical_source -> L.diffuse_reflectance -> L.beer_lambert -> L.detector_response -> L.chromophore_unmixing -> L.threshold_classifier -> int.spatial -> int.spectral
coupling_constraints: fluence_to_absorption: inherited from L1-511 analytical core — multiplicative coupling between mu_a and detected intensity through diffuse-reflectance fluence kernel

W.regime: Existence and uniqueness inherited from L1-511 analytical core; threshold function is piecewise-constant and Lipschitz-continuous on the underlying chromophore-concentration state, so existence and uniqueness lift from continuous to discrete readout under the standard categorical-readout argument (PWM_GATE_CLASSES_AND_SCALING.md Case A). Stability inherits L1-511's kappa_eff ~ 15 plus a small additive contribution C_threshold * |threshold_calibration_uncertainty| from per-class threshold-value uncertainty. The threshold-continuity_proof in discrete_readout demonstrates that boundaries between class regions form measure-zero hyperplanes in chromophore space, so misclassification probability scales linearly with chromophore-recovery error away from the boundary.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [L1-511 chromophore unmixing] + categorical-readout [argmax / smoothed-softmax threshold] | end-to-end deep neural prior [PillCamNet, GINet] with explicit physics-informed analytical-core regularization
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-511 data-fidelity bound for chromophore-concentration recovery: ||c_hat - c||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_wavelengths * SNR_per_band). Misclassification probability bound: Pr(label_hat != label) <= C_thresh * ||c_hat - c||_2 / d(c, threshold_boundary) where d(.) is distance to nearest class boundary in chromophore space. Threshold-calibration-induced bias adds + C_calib * |threshold_calibration_uncertainty|. Achieves q=2 convergence on the chromophore stage; classification stage inherits the underlying convergence rate plus a constant calibration floor.
C.complexity: O(H * W * N_wavelengths * N_chromophores) for stage 1 (inherited from L1-511) + O(H * W * N_classes) for stage 2 threshold evaluation; total ~stage-1-dominated

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Classify capsule frames into {normal, bleeding, polyp, ...}' (would be data_driven_statistical with empirical CNN). Option-3 recast: 'Recover continuous tissue optical state (mu_a, mu_s', chromophores) via diffuse-reflectance physics; apply deterministic threshold to chromophore values to produce categorical readout' — this is the physical_with_discrete_readout class authored here. Recast is feasible because mucosal optical properties have a well-defined physical forward model (radiative transfer / diffusion) and the clinical labels (bleeding, polyp, etc.) are deterministic functions of the underlying physical state.

joint_well_posedness_references: Bjorgan A, Milanic M, Randeberg LL (2014) Estimation of skin optical parameters for real-time hyperspectral imaging applications. J Biomed Opt 19(6):066003.
Calin MA, Parasca SV, Savastru R, Manea D (2014) Hyperspectral imaging in the medical field: present and future. Appl Spectrosc Rev 49(6):435-447.
Akarcay HG et al. (2014) Optical capsule for in vivo gastrointestinal imaging: Monte Carlo simulation. Biomed Opt Express 5(2):412-426.
Yudovsky D, Pilon L (2010) Rapid and accurate estimation of blood saturation, melanin content, and epidermis thickness from spectral diffuse reflectance. Appl Opt 49(10):1707-1719.
Lu G, Fei B (2014) Medical hyperspectral imaging: a review. J Biomed Opt 19(1):010901.
Iddan G, Meron G, Glukhovsky A, Swain P (2000) Wireless capsule endoscopy. Nature 405(6785):417 (foundational PillCam reference).
PWM_GATE_CLASSES_AND_SCALING.md Case A — physically-grounded recast pattern (the canonical PWDR formulation).

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-511 is the analytical core (recovers continuous chromophore concentrations); L1-512 is the PWDR wrapper that applies a deterministic threshold to produce categorical labels. They are paired by design — L1-511 has gate_class='analytical' and L1-512 has gate_class='physical_with_discrete_readout' with physics_core_principle='L1-511'. d_principle ~ 0.35 reflects that they share most physics primitives (L.optical_source, L.diffuse_reflectance, L.beer_lambert, L.detector_response, L.chromophore_unmixing) but L1-512 adds L.threshold_classifier and a fundamentally different solution_space (2D_categorical_mucosal_label_map vs L1-511's 2D_chromophore_concentration). Pair-authoring is the canonical Option-3 recast pattern from PWM_GATE_CLASSES_AND_SCALING.md.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Clinical capsule endoscopy is FDA-cleared for occult GI bleeding evaluation, Crohn's surveillance, NSAID enteropathy, and obstetric bleeding. Multispectral / hyperspectral PillCam variants extend reading-friendly diagnostic value via automated bleeding / polyp / neoplasia detection. Director's GI_Multi_Task active research project aligns directly. Pre-clinical to early clinical; current commercial monochrome systems include Medtronic PillCam SB3 / Crohn's, Olympus Endocapsule 10, Intromedic MiroCam — multispectral upgrades pending. PWDR formulation (L1-511 + L1-512) provides the physics-grounded foundation for AI-assisted reading: discrimination is performed on physics-derived chromophore quantities rather than raw pixel intensities, giving interpretable thresholds and inheriting analytical S1-S4 certificates.
